input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10. Let me provide guidance on this.

In .NET MAUI, the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed in newer versions. The recommended approach for .NET 10 (and starting from .NET 9 in some cases) is to use the `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior or handle it differently on Android.

Actually, let me think about this more carefully. 

The issue is that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` relied on Android's `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` flag, which was deprecated starting in Android 11. .NET MAUI moved away from this approach and now recommends using `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` for automatic keyboard avoidance, or alternatively configuring edge-to-edge display settings. For a chat screen, the key is structuring the layout so the message list expands to fill available space while the input stays anchored at the bottom, then relying on `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` to handle the scrolling automatically—but if that doesn't work as expected, I'd need to manually manage window insets instead.
